channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Everything new on Disney Plus in January 2025
item.description: From the first Marvel TV show of the year to lots of new movies, here's your definitive guide to Disney Plus for January 2025.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Find out who’s behind any phone number with this free lookup tool
item.description: I don’t know about you, but practically every time my phone rings, my heart rate starts skyrocketing.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok creators left in limbo while awaiting decision on potential platform ban
item.description: The fate of TikTok is keeping creators and small business owners in anxious limbo as they await a decision from the Supreme Court that could upheld their livelihoods.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Landrum Jr. And Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 3 hidden reasons you keep running out of iCloud storage
item.description: Apple gives every iCloud user 5GB of free storage space. This storage space can be used for anything—from backing up your iPhone online to saving documents in the cloud to syncing photos across devices.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Ditch boring emoji and create your own unique versions with Genmoji on iPhone
item.description: Genmoji, the latest addition to Apple's Apple Intelligence suite, uses artificial intelligence to generate customized emoji based on text descriptions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dating Wrapped: TikTok users are crunching the numbers on their dating life
item.description: If Spotify Wrapped left you underwhelmed this year, TikTok’s “Dating Wrapped” trend is here to spice things up. Instead of playlists, users are serving up PowerPoint-style presentations packed with stats about their 2024 dating lives.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Inside Pinterest’s quest to restore internet optimism
item.description: Most social media platforms have devolved into a network of bottom-feeders and rage-baiters. Yet somehow, Pinterest has kept its joy. According to Sprout Social’s 2024 content strategy report, more than half of social users think of Pinterest as “more positive” than other platforms.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: My 2025 tech resolutions: ditching X, avoiding AI, and more
item.description: So many of 2024’s tech plot twists defied predictions, but we should have seen one thing coming: The industry would once again pivot away from learning from its mistakes.
item.dc:creator: Rob Pegoraro, Fast Company

item.title: Apple needs good AI acquisition hires
item.description: When it comes to artificial intelligence development, Apple seems more interested than usual in seeking out good acquisition targets, potentially building on the small acquisitions it makes most months. The company has, in fact, led the industry when it comes to strategic AI acquisitions since 2023.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: I love the look of these dynamic wallpapers that were supposedly for Windows 11 – but the idea has been scrapped
item.description: Windows 11’s rumored dynamic wallpapers look seriously smart – but they’ll never brighten your desktop sadly.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: CES 2025: Hisense L9Q Laser TV Unveiled with 100-150 Inch 4K Projection and Dolby Atmos Speakers
item.description: Hisense has unveiled its new laser TV, the L9Q, at CES 2025. The TV features a 4K projector that can deliver up to 150 inches of picture size, a new-gen light-rejecting projector screen in five sizes, and Dolby Atmos/DTS:X support with 6.2.2 built-in speaker channels.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Easy internet sleuth tricks you’ll wish you knew sooner
item.description: Here are some powerful online sleuthing tricks you can use to check the credibility of a website, see old pictures of places you've lived and even learn more about your neighbors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI, power availability and Intel’s future
item.description: As 2025 kicks off, it’s time to dust off the crystal ball. Here are my data center predictions for the coming year. 1) Trump backs off on tech tariffs For all the handwringing, Trump has a history of not following through on his threats.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nearly a year since launch, Apple’s Vision Pro still searching for a killer app
item.description: Apple launched its highly anticipated Vision Pro “spatial computing” headset last Feb. 2 amid significant hype — and hopes it could finally push virtual or mixed reality into mainstream use.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tractor Supply’s Rob Mills on the CIO as chief growth officer
item.description: Rob Mills has been instrumental in driving Tractor Supply Co.’s rapid growth, profitability, and world-class customer experience. Mills joined Tractor Supply a decade ago as its CIO, and within four years, his role, influence, and impact had expanded significantly.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Ser CIO en 2025: estos serán los principales desafíos tecnológicos
item.description: Anticipar cuáles serán las tendencias en tecnología para el próximo año tiene un poco de bola de cristal: su propio carácter disruptivo hace que en ocasiones entre en juego una herramienta que hasta entonces no había destacado.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 4 keys for writing cross-platform apps
item.description: These days, most every application has the potential to be a cross-platform application. That’s great for users but often difficult for developers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Intro to VSCode.dev: The IDE in your browser
item.description: The fully realized browser-based IDE has been a long time coming. Ever since the beginning of the cloud revolution, I’ve thought a powerful and flexible remote IDE was just around the corner. I imagined a development tool that could use any virtual machine as a development platform.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The biggest Wi-Fi mistake you didn’t know you were making
item.description: There are many untapped safeguards and tips you can learn to take your Wi-Fi knowledge to the next level, technology expert Kim Komando shares her tricks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Venezuela's top court issues a $10 million fine for TikTok over allegedly deadly video challenges
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuela’s Supreme Court on Monday issued a $10 million fine against TikTok for “not implementing measures” to prevent viral video challenges that have allegedly led to the deaths of three Venezuelan children recently.
item.dc:creator: JUAN PABLO ARRAEZ and MANUEL RUEDA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rhode Islanders' data was leaked from a cyberattack on state health benefits website
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (AP) — Cybercriminals who hacked Rhode Island’s system for health and benefits programs have released files to a site on the dark web, a scenario the state has been preparing for, Gov. Daniel McKee said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
